en: These are all ways to talk about leaving your job but what's the difference
ko: 이건 다 직장을 떠나는 걸 말하는데 차이가 뭘까?

en: You resign or quit when you decide to leave your job maybe because you have another one I'm
ko: 다른 직장이 있어서 스스로 떠나기로 하면 resign이나 quit을 써.

en: going to resign from my job and become a cake maker You retire when you leave your
ko: 직장을 resign하고 케이크 만드는 사람이 될 거야. 나이나 건강 때문에 직장을 떠나면

en: job because of your age or health My parents retired in their 60s and now they're travelling
ko: retire을 써. 부모님은 60대에 은퇴해서 지금 세계 여행 중이야.

en: the world If you are fired or sacked you are forced to leave your job because you did something wrong
ko: 잘못했거나 일을 못 해서 강제로 떠나게 되면 fired나 sacked야.

en: or you did your job badly I've been sacked from my job as a cake maker
Apparently my cakes are not very good If you're made redundant you're also forced to leave your
ko: 내 케이크가 별로 맛없나 봐. 회사가 문을 닫거나 급여를 줄 여력이 없어서 강제로 떠나게

en: job but this time it's not because you did something wrong Maybe the company is closing or can't
ko: 되면 made redundant야. 하지만 네 잘못은 아니야.

en: afford to pay you anymore We've all been made redundant Apparently the company's closing down Note
ko: 우리 모두 redundant됐어. 회사가 문 닫는대. 참고로

en: the first two are in the active form because you decide to leave The second two are more
ko: 처음 두 개는 네가 떠나기로 한 거라 능동태야. 나머지 두 개는 더

en: commonly used in the passive because someone makes you leave The action is done to
ko: 흔히 수동태로 쓰여, 누군가가 너를 떠나게 하니까. 행위가 너에게

en: you Now fill in the gap with the correct word
ko: 행해지는 거야. 이제 빈칸에 맞는 단어를 넣어봐.
